task_yaml.task:
I'm prepping the reading list for our robotics reading group and I
realized a few papers got mis-filed into the folder at
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/arxiv_papers/. Some have titles that
sound robotics-adjacent but the actual content is a totally different
field, so titles alone won't cut it. I need you to read through each
PDF with the workspace's PDF parser skill, use the
document processing skill to write the summary report, and the Excel creation skill
to produce the spreadsheet. Figure out which papers are genuinely about
robotics or embodied AI based on the actual content, and separate the
mis-filed ones into an off-topic group.

Two outputs:
  1. /tmp_workspace/results/metadata.xlsx — a sheet called "Papers"
     with one row per genuinely-robotics paper, covering the arxiv id,
     title, primary authors, year, abstract, page count, and a BibTeX
     citation. The mis-filed papers must NOT appear as rows in this
     sheet.
  2. /tmp_workspace/results/summary.md — one bullet per
     genuinely-robotics paper of the form
     "- [arxiv_id] title: 1-sentence takeaway", followed by a clearly
     labelled "Excluded" or "Off-topic" section that lists every
     mis-filed paper by arxiv id with a one-line reason explaining why
     it does not belong with the robotics set.
